2605.21801.title: Why Semantic Entropy Fails: Geometry-Aware and Calibrated Uncertainty for Policy Optimization
2605.21801.paper_summary: Post-training has become central to improving reasoning and alignment in large language models, where critic-free models enable scalable learning from model-generated outputs but lack principled mechanisms to distinguish informative from noisy signals. Recent approaches leverage response-level measures as uncertainty signals to regulate group-based optimization methods such as GRPO. Yet their empirical success remains unstable and unclear in how they influence optimization dynamics. In this paper, we provide, to our knowledge, the first principled formulation that interprets uncertainty signals as mechanisms for characterizing and regulating gradient variance and learning signal quality. Based on both empirical and theoretical analysis, we identify two critical gaps of current entropy-based estimators: The anisotropic gap and The calibration gap. Motivated by this analysis, we propose Geometric-aware Calibrated Policy Optimization (GCPO), a novel framework integrating geometry-aware measures to capture semantic disagreement with reward-based calibration to align uncertainty with learning signal strength. Experiments on multiple benchmarks show that our approach more faithfully tracks gradient variability and consistently improves post-training performance. Our results highlight the importance of designing uncertainty signals that are aligned with optimization dynamics, offering a principled perspective for robust post-training.
2605.21801.paper_summary_zh: 後訓練已成為改善大型語言模型推理和對齊的核心，其中無批評模型能夠從模型生成的輸出中進行可擴展學習，但缺乏區分有用信號與噪音信號的原則性機制。最近的方法利用響應級別的度量作為不確定性信號，以調節基於群體的優化方法，如GRPO。然而，它們的實證成功仍然不穩定，且不清楚它們如何影響優化動態。在本文中，我們提供了我們所知的第一個原則性公式，將不確定性信號解釋為表徵和調節梯度變異性及學習信號質量的機制。基於實證和理論分析，我們確定了當前基於熵的估計器的兩個關鍵缺口：各向異性缺口和校準缺口。受到這一分析的啟發，我們提出了幾何感知校準政策優化（GCPO），這是一個新穎的框架，整合幾何感知度量，以捕捉語義不一致性，並通過基於獎勵的校準將不確定性與學習信號強度對齊。在多個基準上的實驗顯示，我們的方法更忠實地追蹤梯度變異性，並持續改善後訓練性能。我們的結果突顯了設計與優化動態對齊的不確定性信號的重要性，為穩健的後訓練提供了一個原則性的視角。
2605.21801.author: Zheyuan Zhang et.al.
2605.21801.authors: Zheyuan Zhang, Kaiwen Shi, Han Bao, Zehong Wang, Tianyi Ma, Yanfang Ye
